zotero.abstractNote: Compositional Zero-Shot Learning (CZSL) aims to recognize unseen compositions of state and object primitives. Existing methods typically learn a joint embedding space for state-object pairs, often ignoring the semantic relationships between primitives. In this paper, we propose a novel approach that learns graph embeddings for CZSL. We construct a graph where nodes represent state and object primitives, and edges represent their semantic relationships. We then use a graph convolutional network to learn embeddings for these primitives, which are then used to predict the state-object composition. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets, demonstrating the effectiveness of leveraging semantic relationships for CZSL.

extra.Conference Name: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
extra.Proceedings Title: Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
